<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
makeDb() requires better-sqlite3 and Drizzle migrations.

S2 is not yet implemented, so the repository it needs is missing. makeDb cannot be fully functional until S2 is ready.

The helpers must be defined and usable now, even though lib/db does not yet exist. A minimal inline schema may be required for makeDb to function. The smoke test likely imports each helper and verifies basic calls such as formData and renderWithPersona.

makeDb must return a typed repository. This requires bundling S2 schema, migrations, and repository logic into S7-setup. Otherwise the helper would throw until S2 exists, violating T31. The with-vitest Next.js example may provide useful patterns.

makeDb could be a lightweight factory returning a generic Drizzle database handle rather than a full typed repository. The T31 smoke test assertions need to be written first per TDD. T30 requires the vitest harness to boot successfully and exit cleanly.

I will create vitest.config.mts and install the required dependencies. makeDb will be implemented minimally inside tests/_helpers/makeDb.ts. It will open an in-memory SQLite database. It will apply the CREATE TABLE statement inline. It will return a database handle with stub repository methods.

S7-setup must stay strictly within its single tech-spec section and cannot implement S2. makeDb will live in tests/_helpers/ and import from lib/db once that module exists. For T31, it will either run migrations against an existing lib/db client or embed its own minimal migration SQL directly in the helper.

makeDb will be placed in tests/_helpers/ and will run Drizzle migrations from the S2 drizzle/ folder. Creating a minimal lib/db module solely for makeDb would bleed S2 scope. Instead, makeDb will apply raw SQL from a test-only migration file inside tests/_helpers/ and return a minimal repository interface.

T31 likely only requires makeDb to be defined and return a non-null value, not a fully typed repository. Using inline schema pushes or reading the S2 drizzle/ migrations folder would duplicate S2 work. The helper should remain minimal and avoid importing S2 types.

makeDb will include the full S2 schema because the helper itself must bootstrap the database. S2 will later provide the production lib/db/client.ts and lib/db/snapshots.ts modules. makeDb will wrap or reuse those production pieces. A minimal lib/db/schema.ts will be created for S7-setup.</details></think>

      "chunk": "Links:\n1. [chaance/vitest-axe](https://github.com/chaance/vitest-axe)\n2. [vitest-axe - npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/vitest-axe)\n3. [Usage Guide | chaance/vitest-axe | DeepWiki](https://deepwiki.com/chaance/vitest-axe/4-usage-guide)\n4. [Readme](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/vitest-axe@0.1.0/README.md)\n5. [vitest-axe | RNA](https://chialab.github.io/rna/guide/vitest-axe.html)\n\nSynthesis:\nTo set up accessibility testing with &#96;vitest-axe&#96;, install the package as a development dependency [1][2]. The simplest integration is to import the library's &#96;extend-expect&#96; module in your Vitest setup file [1][3].\n\nIn your setup file (e.g., &#96;vitest-setup.ts&#96;):\nimport \"vitest-axe/extend-expect\";\n\nEnsure your setup file is referenced in your &#96;vitest.config.ts&#96; under the &#96;setupFiles&#96; array and included in your &#96;tsconfig.json&#96; [2][4]. Note that &#96;vitest-axe&#96; is incompatible with &#96;happy-dom&#96; due to a known &#96;isConnected&#96; property bug; use &#96;jsdom&#96; instead [1].\n\nHighlights:\n\u003cresult id=\"1\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003echaance/vitest-axe\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://github.com/chaance/vitest-axe\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e### Import &#96;vitest-axe/extend-expect&#96; module\n[...]\nThe simplest way to use this library is to import &#96;vitest-axe/extend-expect&#96;\nfrom your [test setup file][test setup file].\n[...]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;js\n// vitest-setup.js\nimport \"vitest-axe/extend-expect\";\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n[...]\n### Extend in test setup file\n[...]\nYou can also import the matchers from &#96;vitest-axe/matchers&#96; then pass them to\nVitest's &#96;expect.extend&#96; method yourself:\n[...]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;js\n// vitest-setup.js\nimport * as matchers from \"vitest-axe/matchers\";\nimport { expect } from \"vitest\";\nexpect.extend(matchers);\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n[...]\n### Extend in individual tests\n[...]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ts\nimport { expect } from \"vitest\";\nimport { axe } from \"vitest-axe\";\nimport { toHaveNoViolations } from \"vitest-axe/matchers\";\n\nexpect.extend({ toHaveNoViolations });\n\nit(\"should have no axe violations\", async () =\u003e {\n\tconst html = \"\u003chtml\u003e\u003c!-- accessible markup! --\u003e\u003c/html\u003e\";\n\texpect(await axe(html)).toHaveNoViolations();\n});\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n[...]\n### With TypeScript\n[...]\nIf you [imported the &#96;vitest/extend-expect&#96; module](#import-vitest-axeextend-expect-module)\nin your setup file, you should be good to go. Make sure your setup file is\n[included in your &#96;tsconfig.json&#96;](https://www.typescriptlang.org/tsconfig#include).\n\nIf you do not import the &#96;vitest/extend-expect&#96; module, you will need to augment Vitest's &#96;Assertion&#96; and &#96;AsymmetricMatchersContaining&#96; interfaces.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ts\nimport \"vitest\";\nimport type { AxeMatchers } from \"vitest-axe/matchers\";\n\ndeclare module \"vitest\" {\n\texport interface Assertion extends AxeMatchers {}\n\texport interface AsymmetricMatchersContaining extends AxeMatchers {}\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n[...]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;ts\nimport { axe } from \"vitest-axe\";\n[...]\nit(\"should demonstrate this matcher's usage\", async () =\u003e {\n\tconst render = () =\u003e '\u003cimg src=\"#\"/\u003e';\n\t// pass anything that outputs html to axe\n\tconst html = render();\n\texpect(await axe(html)).toHaveNoViolations();\n});\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n \n[vitest\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"2\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003evitest-axe - npm\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.npmjs.com/package/vitest-axe\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003evitest-axe - npm\n\n# vitest-axe\n\n0.1.0 • Public • Published 4 years ago\n\n# vitest-axe\n\nCustom Vitest matcher for testing accessibility with aXe\n\n---\n\nThis library is a fork of jest-axe. It shares that library's implementation and API. It is intended to make it easier to include its matchers without clashes between Vitest and Jest's environment or types.\n\nSee the README for the original package for usage details.\n\n## Installation\n\nThis module should be installed as one of your project's&#96;devDependencies&#96;:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n# with npm\nnpm install --save-dev vitest-axe\n# yarn\nyarn add --dev vitest-axe\n# pnpm\npnpm add --dev vitest-axe\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Usage\n\nImport the matchers from&#96;vitest-axe/matchers&#96; once (perferably in your tests setup file), then pass them to Vitest's&#96;expect.extend&#96; method:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n// vitest-setup.js\nimport * as matchers from \"vitest-axe/matchers\";\nimport { expect } from \"vitest\";\nexpect.extend(matchers);\n\n// vitest.config.js\nexport default defineConfig({\n  test: {\n    setupFiles: [\"vitest-setup.js\"],\n  },\n});\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### With TypeScript\n\nIf you're using TypeScript, make sure your setup file is a&#96;.ts&#96; and not a&#96;.js&#96; to include the necessary types. Importing from&#96;vitest-axe/extend-expect&#96; will add the matchers to Vitest's&#96;expect&#96; types.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n// vitest-setup.ts\nimport \"vitest-axe/extend-expect\";\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nYou will also need to include your setup file in your&#96;tsconfig.json&#96; if you haven't already:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n  // In tsconfig.json\n  \"include\": [\n    // ...\n    \"./vitest-setup.ts\"\n  ],\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Readme\n\n### Keywords\n\nnone\n\nViewing vitest-axe version 0.1.0\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"3\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eUsage Guide | chaance/vitest-axe | DeepWiki\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://deepwiki.com/chaance/vitest-axe/4-usage-guide\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e## Setup Options\n[...]\nThere are several ways to set up vitest-axe in your project:\n[...]\n### Option 1: Import extend-expect module\n[...]\nThe simplest approach is to import the &#96;vitest-axe/extend-expect&#96; module in your Vitest setup file:\n[...]\nThis automatically extends Vitest's &#96;expect&#96; function with the custom matchers from vitest-axe.\n[...]\n### Option 2: Manually extend Vitest's expect\n[...]\nYou can also import the matchers separately and manually extend Vitest's &#96;expect&#96;:\n[...]\n\u003cdiv class\n[...]\n5px]\" style=\"background\n[...]\n:2em\"\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n[...]\n### Option 3: Extend in individual tests\n[...]\nFor more granular control, you can extend &#96;expect&#96; within individual test files:\n[...]\n\u003cdiv class=\"\n[...]\nF8F8\n[...]\n]/30 p-2 text-\n[...]\n-[15px]\" style=\"background-color:\n[...]\n;min-height:2em\"\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n[...]\n## TypeScript Integration\n[...]\n### With extend-expect\n[...]\nIf you import the &#96;vitest-axe/extend-expect&#96; module in your setup file, TypeScript should automatically recognize the added matchers, provided your setup file is included in your &#96;tsconfig.json&#96;.\n[...]\n### Manual type augmentation\n[...]\nIf you don't use the &#96;extend-expect&#96; module, you need to manually augment Vitest's interfaces:\n[...]\n\u003cdiv class=\"\n[...]\np-2\n[...]\nNoViolations\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"4\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eReadme\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/vitest-axe@0.1.0/README.md\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003evitest-axe\n\nCustom Vitest matcher for testing accessibility with aXe\n\n---\n\nThis library is a fork of &#96;jest-axe&#96;.\nIt shares that library's implementation and API. It is intended to make it\neasier to include its matchers without clashes between Vitest and\nJest's environment or types.\n\nSee the &#96;README&#96; for the original package for usage details.\n\n## Installation\n\nThis module should be installed as one of your project's &#96;devDependencies&#96;:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;shell\n# with npm\nnpm install --save-dev vitest-axe\n# yarn\nyarn add --dev vitest-axe\n# pnpm\npnpm add --dev vitest-axe\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Usage\n\nImport the matchers from &#96;vitest-axe/matchers&#96; once (perferably in your tests\nsetup file), then pass them to Vitest's &#96;expect.extend&#96; method:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;javascript\n// vitest-setup.js\nimport * as matchers from \"vitest-axe/matchers\";\nimport { expect } from \"vitest\";\nexpect.extend(matchers);\n\n// vitest.config.js\nexport default defineConfig({\n  test: {\n    setupFiles: [\"vitest-setup.js\"],\n  },\n});\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### With TypeScript\n\nIf you're using TypeScript, make sure your setup file is a &#96;.ts&#96; and not a &#96;.js&#96;\nto include the necessary types. Importing from &#96;vitest-axe/extend-expect&#96; will\nadd the matchers to Vitest's &#96;expect&#96; types.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\n// vitest-setup.ts\nimport \"vitest-axe/extend-expect\";\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nYou will also need to include your setup file in your &#96;tsconfig.json&#96; if you\nhaven't already:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json5\n  // In tsconfig.json\n  \"include\": [\n    // ...\n    \"./vitest-setup.ts\"\n  ],\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"5\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003evitest-axe | RNA\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://chialab.github.io/rna/guide/vitest-axe.html\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003evitest-axe | RNA\n\n# vitest-axe ​\n\nAxe matchers for Vitest.\n\n## Install ​\n\nnpmyarnpnpm\n\nsh\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nnpm i -D axe-core @chialab/vitest-axe\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nsh\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nyarn add -D axe-core @chialab/vitest-axe\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nsh\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\npnpm add -D axe-core @chialab/vitest-axe\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Usage ​\n\nUse a Vitest setup file to add the matchers to the test runner expectation API. Also, make sure to include typings for the matchers in the&#96;tsconfig.json&#96; file.\n\nvitest.config.tstest/setup.tstsconfig.json\n\nts\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nexport default {\n    test: {\n        setupFiles: ['./test/setup.ts'],\n    },\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nts\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nimport matchers from '@chialab/vitest-axe';\nimport { expect } from 'vitest';\n\nexpect.extend(matchers);\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\njson\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n{\n    \"compilerOptions\": {\n        // ...\n        \"types\": [\"@chialab/vitest-axe/matchers\"]\n    }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Example ​\n\nts\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nimport { run as axe } from 'axe-core';\nimport { describe, expect, test } from 'vitest';\n\ndescribe('button', () =\u003e {\n    test('accessibility', async () =\u003e {\n        const button = document.createElement('button');\n        expect(await axe(button)).toHaveNoViolations();\n    });\n});\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e",

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
A clear plan is ready. Implementation begins now.

Vitest setup files and configuration will be created next. Package.json scripts will be updated. Helper utilities and test files for T30 and T31 are planned. T30 will verify the harness boots and loads plugins. A minimal database helper using better-sqlite3 and Drizzle ORM will handle inline migrations.

The `makeDb` helper will return a Drizzle instance and a `close` method. It creates an in-memory SQLite database and runs a basic `snapshots` table migration. Repository methods will be stubbed since S1 and S2 types are unavailable.

T31 will verify that `makeDb()` returns a database instance and that `close()` works. A `withPersonaCookie` helper will stub `next/headers` cookies for persona ID lookup. `vi.mock` is hoisted in Vitest and must be placed at the top level.

The `withPersonaCookie` helper is being reworked. It will likely return `{ setup, teardown }` pairs for `beforeEach` and `afterEach`, using `vi.doMock` or `vi.spyOn` to restore the real `next/headers` module between tests. A dynamic `mockPersonaCookie` implementation is being drafted with `vi.fn` for the mocked `cookies` function.

The `withPersonaCookie` helper is being refined to return an `install` method instead of a raw callback. This avoids hoisting issues with `vi.mock` by allowing the mock to be applied inside `beforeEach` blocks.

The `withPersonaCookie` helper will return a context object with `getCookiesMock` and `restore` methods. It will use `vi.hoisted` and `vi.mock` to apply the `next/headers` stub dynamically.

The `withPersonaCookie` helper is being simplified further. It may return a stub cookie reader function instead of a full context object. A shared mutable variable will track the current persona ID. This matches the S5 server action test pattern where `withPersonaCookie('jordan')` is called in `beforeEach`.

The `withPersonaCookie` helper is finalized as a function that mutates a shared `currentPersonaId` variable and returns a cleanup function. The `vi.mock` stub must reside in a helper file imported before the module under test. T31 will smoke-test this by setting a persona, asserting the mock behaves correctly, then restoring the original state.

`renderWithPersona` will wrap `@testing-library/react`'s `render` for React components. It will accept a `ReactElement` and a `personaId` string. No extra providers are required yet.

`forbiddenToneTokens` will be exported as a combined array of tone and advice-implying tokens. Separate exports for each list are also being considered.

T30 will be a minimal harness test. It verifies the runner exits zero when no application tests exist. T31 will smoke-test the database helper. These two tests will prove the infrastructure works before application tests are added.

T30 will import the Vitest config directly and assert that the environment is jsdom and plugins are loaded.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

**S7-setup is done** (session S009). Pre-checks passed; PRD and TECH_SPEC were clean on `2c8d063`.

## Scope delivered

| ID | What shipped |
|---|---|
| **R4** | Test infrastructure for all later slices |
| **T30** | Vitest harness (jsdom, plugins, scripts, vitest-axe) |
| **T31** | Shared helpers under `tests/_helpers/` |

## What was added

- **`vitest.config.mts`** — jsdom, `@vitejs/plugin-react`, `vite-tsconfig-paths`, setup file
- **`vitest-setup.ts`** + **`vitest-axe.d.ts`** — axe matchers (`vitest-axe/extend-expect` is empty in v0.1.0, so matchers are registered manually)
- **`package.json`** — `test` (`vitest run`), `test:watch` (`vitest`)
- **`tests/_helpers/`** — `makeDb`, `formData`, `withPersonaCookie`, `renderWithPersona`, `forbiddenToneTokens` (TEST_PLAN §2.2 lists)
- **`tests/s7-setup/`** — T30 and T31 test files

## Verification

```bash
npm test          # 7 passed
npm run typecheck # pass
```

`npm run lint` still fails on a pre-existing `.claude/settings.json` format issue (outside this slice).

## Note on `makeDb()`

It opens an in-memory SQLite DB with the S2 snapshots schema and returns a Drizzle handle. Full repository methods come in **`/implement S2`**.

## Next step

Run **`/implement S1`** for the affordability domain (T1–T14, T18, T29).

Session snapshot: `docs/ai/sessions/S009-implement-s7-setup.md`. Changes are uncommitted — say if you want a commit.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The test plan specifies unit tests under `lib/**/*.test.ts` and `tests/_helpers/`.

`tests/s7-setup/` is not required by the spec. It was added to mirror slice IDs for traceability. After the one-time infrastructure work is done, these tests rarely change. They could be moved to `tests/_helpers/` instead.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

**No — `tests/s7-setup/` is not required by the spec.**

What the docs actually pin down:

- **Helpers** → `tests/_helpers/` (TECH_SPEC, TEST_PLAN)
- **T30 / T31** → must exist and pass; placement is not specified
- **Later unit tests** → mostly `lib/**/*.test.ts` (TEST_PLAN §1 layer table)

`tests/s7-setup/` was a slice traceability choice: folder name matches `S7-setup`, and files map cleanly to T30/T31 for the `/implement` audit. It’s convention, not a gate requirement.

**Practical options:**

| Approach | Pros |
|---|---|
| **Keep `tests/s7-setup/`** | Easy to see “infra slice” tests; matches session docs |
| **Collapse** — e.g. `tests/_helpers/helpers.test.tsx` (T31) + `tests/harness.test.ts` (T30) | Fewer folders; aligns with TEST_PLAN’s `tests/_helpers/` mention |
| **Single smoke file** — one `tests/_helpers/smoke.test.ts` covering both | Minimal; fine since S7-setup is one-time setup |

After S7-setup lands, that folder won’t grow — S1+ tests will live under `lib/` and component paths. So it’s optional scaffolding you can flatten without losing coverage.

If you want it gone, I can move T30/T31 into `tests/_helpers/` (or a single smoke file) and delete `tests/s7-setup/`.
